headline: Agent-native development tooling dominated the quarter as resilience techniques broke out as new entrants.
body: Agent-native development held the lead this window, with Cursor reporting that 95% of its usage is now agents and announcing a model trained from scratch alongside cloud agent updates, and with Hermes shipping a Blank Slate mode and an Agent Operating System for building teams of autonomous sub-agents. Local inference advanced in parallel, as a Gemma demonstration ran 10 subagents on a single machine above 170 tokens per second with orchestrator-and-subagent delegation, and NotebookLM moved toward autonomous code and document outputs. The sharpest rotation came from a cluster of resilience techniques entering as new breakout entrants: data backup, immutability, disaster recovery, and ransomware protection. Privacy-preserving inference and encrypted reasoning also carried the highest momentum ratios among lagging movers.
